title: Add Windows-native local runtime scripts and env templates
verify: Windows-native local runtime scripts start or verify PostgreSQL, backend, and storefront without Docker containers.
Local env templates exist and real .env files are ignored.
Services start or verify locally without production credentials.

verify.summary: Windows-native local runtime verification passed. check:local-env confirmed Windows 10, Node/npm, PostgreSQL 18.4, env templates, ignored real .env files, and dockerRequired:false. dev:local confirmed bounded startup readiness for backend/storefront commands. smoke:local verified backend DB migration/seed/read-write smoke plus backend and storefront typecheck.

evidence_required: Windows-native startup/smoke output showing required services.
Evidence that .env templates are committed and real .env files are ignored.
constraints: Local development must not require Docker containers.
No remote server deploy target is introduced.
Provider settings must use placeholders or mock/local values only.
invariants: No production secrets may be committed.
Do not introduce microservices or Docker containers for local development.
purpose: Provide the Windows-native local runtime path required by REQ-030.
success_outcome: A developer can start or verify local PostgreSQL, backend, and storefront on Windows 10 without Docker containers using non-secret env templates.
anti_goals: Do not add remote server deployment configuration.
Do not use Docker or Docker Compose for local development.
Do not wire real payment/OAuth/email provider credentials.
Do not replace Medusa Admin with a custom admin.

stop_conditions: Local Windows-native setup needs production secrets to start.
Local runtime cannot verify PostgreSQL, backend, and storefront.
Implementation requires adding an external managed service.
